name: wfuzz
title: wfuzz

commands.name: wfuzz

arguments.description: This help
arguments.raw: -h/--help                 : This help

arguments.description: Advanced help
arguments.raw: --help                    : Advanced help

arguments.description: Filter language specification
arguments.raw: --filter-help             : Filter language specification

arguments.description: Wfuzz version details
arguments.raw: --version                 : Wfuzz version details

arguments.description: List of available encoders/payloads/iterators/printers/scripts
arguments.raw: -e <type>                 : List of available encoders/payloads/iterators/printers/scripts

arguments.description: Reads options from a recipe. Repeat for various recipes.
arguments.raw: --recipe <filename>       : Reads options from a recipe. Repeat for various recipes.

arguments.description: Prints current options as a recipe
arguments.raw: --dump-recipe <filename>  : Prints current options as a recipe

arguments.description: Saves fuzz results to a file. These can be consumed later using the wfuzz payload.
arguments.raw: --oF <filename>           : Saves fuzz results to a file. These can be consumed later using the wfuzz payload.

arguments.description: Output with colors
arguments.raw: -c                        : Output with colors

arguments.description: Verbose information.
arguments.raw: -v                        : Verbose information.

arguments.description: Store results in the output file using the specified printer (raw printer if omitted).
arguments.raw: -f filename,printer       : Store results in the output file using the specified printer (raw printer if omitted).

arguments.description: Show results using the specified printer.
arguments.raw: -o printer                : Show results using the specified printer.

arguments.description: (beta) If selected,all key presses are captured. This allows you to interact with the program.
arguments.raw: --interact                : (beta) If selected,all key presses are captured. This allows you to interact with the program.

arguments.description: Print the results of applying the requests without actually making any HTTP request.
arguments.raw: --dry-run                 : Print the results of applying the requests without actually making any HTTP request.

arguments.description: Print the previous HTTP requests (only when using payloads generating fuzzresults)
arguments.raw: --prev                    : Print the previous HTTP requests (only when using payloads generating fuzzresults)

arguments.description: Show the specified language expression together with the current payload. Repeat for various fields.
arguments.raw: --efield <expr>           : Show the specified language expression together with the current payload. Repeat for various fields.

arguments.description: Do not show the payload but only the specified language expression. Repeat for various fields.
arguments.raw: --field <expr>            : Do not show the payload but only the specified language expression. Repeat for various fields.

arguments.description: Use Proxy in format ip:port:type. Repeat option for using various proxies.
arguments.raw: -p addr                   : Use Proxy in format ip:port:type. Repeat option for using various proxies.

arguments.description: Specify the number of concurrent connections (10 default)
arguments.raw: -t N                      : Specify the number of concurrent connections (10 default)

arguments.description: Specify time delay between requests (0 default)
arguments.raw: -s N                      : Specify time delay between requests (0 default)

arguments.description: Recursive path discovery being depth the maximum recursion level.
arguments.raw: -R depth                  : Recursive path discovery being depth the maximum recursion level.

arguments.description: Maximum link depth level.
arguments.raw: -D depth                  : Maximum link depth level.

arguments.description: Follow HTTP redirections
arguments.raw: -L,--follow               : Follow HTTP redirections

arguments.description: port            : Specify an IP to connect to instead of the URL's host in the format ip:port
arguments.raw: --ip host:port            : Specify an IP to connect to instead of the URL's host in the format ip:port

arguments.description: Scan mode (Connection errors will be ignored).
arguments.raw: -Z                        : Scan mode (Connection errors will be ignored).

arguments.description: Sets the maximum time in seconds the request is allowed to take (CURLOPT_TIMEOUT). Default 90.
arguments.raw: --req-delay N             : Sets the maximum time in seconds the request is allowed to take (CURLOPT_TIMEOUT). Default 90.

arguments.description: Sets the maximum time in seconds the connection phase to the server to take (CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT). Default 90.
arguments.raw: --conn-delay N            : Sets the maximum time in seconds the connection phase to the server to take (CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT). Default 90.

arguments.description: Alias for -v -c and --script=default,verbose,discover respectively
arguments.raw: -A, --AA, --AAA           : Alias for -v -c and --script=default,verbose,discover respectively

arguments.description: Disable plugins cache. Every request will be scanned.
arguments.raw: --no-cache                : Disable plugins cache. Every request will be scanned.

arguments.description: Equivalent to --script=default
arguments.raw: --script=                 : Equivalent to --script=default

arguments.description: Show help about scripts.
arguments.raw: --script-help=<plugins>   : Show help about scripts.

arguments.description: Provide arguments to scripts. ie. --script-args grep.regex="<A href=\"(.*?)\">"
arguments.raw: --script-args n1=v1,...   : Provide arguments to scripts. ie. --script-args grep.regex="<A href=\"(.*?)\">"

arguments.description: Specify a URL for the request.
arguments.raw: -u url                    : Specify a URL for the request.

arguments.description: Specify an iterator for combining payloads (product by default)
arguments.raw: -m iterator               : Specify an iterator for combining payloads (product by default)

arguments.description: Specify a payload for each FUZZ keyword used in the form of name[,parameter][,encoder].
arguments.raw: -z payload                : Specify a payload for each FUZZ keyword used in the form of name[,parameter][,encoder].

arguments.description: Arguments for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
arguments.raw: --zP <params>             : Arguments for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).

arguments.description: Default parameter for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
arguments.default: parameter for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w
arguments.raw: --zD <default>            : Default parameter for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).

arguments.description: Encoder for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
arguments.raw: --zE <encoder>            : Encoder for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).

arguments.description: Filter payload's elements using the specified expression. It must be preceded by -z.
arguments.raw: --slice <filter>          : Filter payload's elements using the specified expression. It must be preceded by -z.

arguments.description: Specify a wordlist file (alias for -z file,wordlist).
arguments.raw: -w wordlist               : Specify a wordlist file (alias for -z file,wordlist).

arguments.description: All parameters bruteforcing (allvars and allpost). No need for FUZZ keyword.
arguments.raw: -V alltype                : All parameters bruteforcing (allvars and allpost). No need for FUZZ keyword.

arguments.description: Specify an HTTP method for the request, ie. HEAD or FUZZ
arguments.raw: -X method                 : Specify an HTTP method for the request, ie. HEAD or FUZZ

arguments.description: Specify a cookie for the requests. Repeat option for various cookies.
arguments.raw: -b cookie                 : Specify a cookie for the requests. Repeat option for various cookies.

arguments.description: Use post data (ex: "id=FUZZ&catalogue=1")
arguments.raw: -d postdata               : Use post data (ex: "id=FUZZ&catalogue=1")

arguments.description: Use header (ex:"Cookie:id=1312321&user=FUZZ"). Repeat option for various headers.
arguments.raw: -H header                 : Use header (ex:"Cookie:id=1312321&user=FUZZ"). Repeat option for various headers.

arguments.description: in format "user:pass" or "FUZZ:FUZZ" or "domain\FUZ2Z:FUZZ"
arguments.raw: --basic/ntlm/digest auth  : in format "user:pass" or "FUZZ:FUZZ" or "domain\FUZ2Z:FUZZ"

arguments.description: Hide responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
arguments.raw: --hc/hl/hw/hh N[,N]+      : Hide responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)

arguments.description: Show responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
arguments.raw: --sc/sl/sw/sh N[,N]+      : Show responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)

arguments.description: Show/hide responses with the specified regex within the content
arguments.raw: --ss/hs regex             : Show/hide responses with the specified regex within the content

arguments.description: Show/hide responses using the specified filter expression (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
arguments.raw: --filter <filter>         : Show/hide responses using the specified filter expression (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)

arguments.description: Filter items before fuzzing using the specified expression. Repeat for concatenating filters.
arguments.raw: --prefilter <filter>      : Filter items before fuzzing using the specified expression. Repeat for concatenating filters.

arguments.description: This help
arguments.raw: -h                        : This help

arguments.description: Alias for --script=default -v -c
arguments.raw: -A     Alias for --script=default -v -c

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wfuzz --help
 ********************************************************
 * Wfuzz 3.1.0 - The Web Fuzzer                         *
 *                                                      *
 * Version up to 1.4c coded by:                         *
 * Christian Martorella (cmartorella@edge-security.com) *
 * Carlos del ojo (deepbit@gmail.com)                   *
 *                                                      *
 * Version 1.4d to 3.1.0 coded by:                      *
 * Xavier Mendez (xmendez@edge-security.com)            *
 ********************************************************
 
 Usage:	wfuzz [options] -z payload,params <url>
 
 	FUZZ, ..., FUZnZ  wherever you put these keywords wfuzz will replace them with the values of the specified payload.
 	FUZZ{baseline_value} FUZZ will be replaced by baseline_value. It will be the first request performed and could be used as a base for filtering.
 
 
 Options:
 	-h/--help                 : This help
 	--help                    : Advanced help
 	--filter-help             : Filter language specification
 	--version                 : Wfuzz version details
 	-e <type>                 : List of available encoders/payloads/iterators/printers/scripts
 	
 	--recipe <filename>       : Reads options from a recipe. Repeat for various recipes.
 	--dump-recipe <filename>  : Prints current options as a recipe
 	--oF <filename>           : Saves fuzz results to a file. These can be consumed later using the wfuzz payload.
 	
 	-c                        : Output with colors
 	-v                        : Verbose information.
 	-f filename,printer       : Store results in the output file using the specified printer (raw printer if omitted).
 	-o printer                : Show results using the specified printer.
 	--interact                : (beta) If selected,all key presses are captured. This allows you to interact with the program.
 	--dry-run                 : Print the results of applying the requests without actually making any HTTP request.
 	--prev                    : Print the previous HTTP requests (only when using payloads generating fuzzresults)
 	--efield <expr>           : Show the specified language expression together with the current payload. Repeat for various fields.
 	--field <expr>            : Do not show the payload but only the specified language expression. Repeat for various fields.
 	
 	-p addr                   : Use Proxy in format ip:port:type. Repeat option for using various proxies.
 	                            Where type could be SOCKS4,SOCKS5 or HTTP if omitted.
 	
 	-t N                      : Specify the number of concurrent connections (10 default)
 	-s N                      : Specify time delay between requests (0 default)
 	-R depth                  : Recursive path discovery being depth the maximum recursion level.
 	-D depth                  : Maximum link depth level.
 	-L,--follow               : Follow HTTP redirections
 	--ip host:port            : Specify an IP to connect to instead of the URL's host in the format ip:port
 	-Z                        : Scan mode (Connection errors will be ignored).
 	--req-delay N             : Sets the maximum time in seconds the request is allowed to take (CURLOPT_TIMEOUT). Default 90.
 	--conn-delay N            : Sets the maximum time in seconds the connection phase to the server to take (CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT). Default 90.
 	
 	-A, --AA, --AAA           : Alias for -v -c and --script=default,verbose,discover respectively
 	--no-cache                : Disable plugins cache. Every request will be scanned.
 	--script=                 : Equivalent to --script=default
 	--script=<plugins>        : Runs script's scan. <plugins> is a comma separated list of plugin-files or plugin-categories
 	--script-help=<plugins>   : Show help about scripts.
 	--script-args n1=v1,...   : Provide arguments to scripts. ie. --script-args grep.regex="<A href=\"(.*?)\">"
 	
 	-u url                    : Specify a URL for the request.
 	-m iterator               : Specify an iterator for combining payloads (product by default)
 	-z payload                : Specify a payload for each FUZZ keyword used in the form of name[,parameter][,encoder].
 	                            A list of encoders can be used, ie. md5-sha1. Encoders can be chained, ie. md5@sha1.
 	                            Encoders category can be used. ie. url
 	                            Use help as a payload to show payload plugin's details (you can filter using --slice)
 	--zP <params>             : Arguments for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
 	--zD <default>            : Default parameter for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
 	--zE <encoder>            : Encoder for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
 	--slice <filter>          : Filter payload's elements using the specified expression. It must be preceded by -z.
 	-w wordlist               : Specify a wordlist file (alias for -z file,wordlist).
 	-V alltype                : All parameters bruteforcing (allvars and allpost). No need for FUZZ keyword.
 	-X method                 : Specify an HTTP method for the request, ie. HEAD or FUZZ
 	
 	-b cookie                 : Specify a cookie for the requests. Repeat option for various cookies.
 	-d postdata               : Use post data (ex: "id=FUZZ&catalogue=1")
 	-H header                 : Use header (ex:"Cookie:id=1312321&user=FUZZ"). Repeat option for various headers.
 	--basic/ntlm/digest auth  : in format "user:pass" or "FUZZ:FUZZ" or "domain\FUZ2Z:FUZZ"
 	
 	--hc/hl/hw/hh N[,N]+      : Hide responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
 	--sc/sl/sw/sh N[,N]+      : Show responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
 	--ss/hs regex             : Show/hide responses with the specified regex within the content
 	--filter <filter>         : Show/hide responses using the specified filter expression (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
 	--prefilter <filter>      : Filter items before fuzzing using the specified expression. Repeat for concatenating filters.

local_help.--help:
default
default
********************************************************
* Wfuzz 3.1.0 - The Web Fuzzer                         *
*                                                      *
* Version up to 1.4c coded by:                         *
* Christian Martorella (cmartorella@edge-security.com) *
* Carlos del ojo (deepbit@gmail.com)                   *
*                                                      *
* Version 1.4d to 3.1.0 coded by:                      *
* Xavier Mendez (xmendez@edge-security.com)            *
********************************************************

Usage:	wfuzz [options] -z payload,params <url>

	FUZZ, ..., FUZnZ  wherever you put these keywords wfuzz will replace them with the values of the specified payload.
	FUZZ{baseline_value} FUZZ will be replaced by baseline_value. It will be the first request performed and could be used as a base for filtering.


Options:
	-h/--help                 : This help
	--help                    : Advanced help
	--filter-help             : Filter language specification
	--version                 : Wfuzz version details
	-e <type>                 : List of available encoders/payloads/iterators/printers/scripts
	
	--recipe <filename>       : Reads options from a recipe. Repeat for various recipes.
	--dump-recipe <filename>  : Prints current options as a recipe
	--oF <filename>           : Saves fuzz results to a file. These can be consumed later using the wfuzz payload.
	
	-c                        : Output with colors
	-v                        : Verbose information.
	-f filename,printer       : Store results in the output file using the specified printer (raw printer if omitted).
	-o printer                : Show results using the specified printer.
	--interact                : (beta) If selected,all key presses are captured. This allows you to interact with the program.
	--dry-run                 : Print the results of applying the requests without actually making any HTTP request.
	--prev                    : Print the previous HTTP requests (only when using payloads generating fuzzresults)
	--efield <expr>           : Show the specified language expression together with the current payload. Repeat for various fields.
	--field <expr>            : Do not show the payload but only the specified language expression. Repeat for various fields.
	
	-p addr                   : Use Proxy in format ip:port:type. Repeat option for using various proxies.
	                            Where type could be SOCKS4,SOCKS5 or HTTP if omitted.
	
	-t N                      : Specify the number of concurrent connections (10 default)
	-s N                      : Specify time delay between requests (0 default)
	-R depth                  : Recursive path discovery being depth the maximum recursion level.
	-D depth                  : Maximum link depth level.
	-L,--follow               : Follow HTTP redirections
	--ip host:port            : Specify an IP to connect to instead of the URL's host in the format ip:port
	-Z                        : Scan mode (Connection errors will be ignored).
	--req-delay N             : Sets the maximum time in seconds the request is allowed to take (CURLOPT_TIMEOUT). Default 90.
	--conn-delay N            : Sets the maximum time in seconds the connection phase to the server to take (CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT). Default 90.
	
	-A, --AA, --AAA           : Alias for -v -c and --script=default,verbose,discover respectively
	--no-cache                : Disable plugins cache. Every request will be scanned.
	--script=                 : Equivalent to --script=default
	--script=<plugins>        : Runs script's scan. <plugins> is a comma separated list of plugin-files or plugin-categories
	--script-help=<plugins>   : Show help about scripts.
	--script-args n1=v1,...   : Provide arguments to scripts. ie. --script-args grep.regex="<A href=\"(.*?)\">"
	
	-u url                    : Specify a URL for the request.
	-m iterator               : Specify an iterator for combining payloads (product by default)
	-z payload                : Specify a payload for each FUZZ keyword used in the form of name[,parameter][,encoder].
	                            A list of encoders can be used, ie. md5-sha1. Encoders can be chained, ie. md5@sha1.
	                            Encoders category can be used. ie. url
	                            Use help as a payload to show payload plugin's details (you can filter using --slice)
	--zP <params>             : Arguments for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
	--zD <default>            : Default parameter for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
	--zE <encoder>            : Encoder for the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or -w).
	--slice <filter>          : Filter payload's elements using the specified expression. It must be preceded by -z.
	-w wordlist               : Specify a wordlist file (alias for -z file,wordlist).
	-V alltype                : All parameters bruteforcing (allvars and allpost). No need for FUZZ keyword.
	-X method                 : Specify an HTTP method for the request, ie. HEAD or FUZZ
	
	-b cookie                 : Specify a cookie for the requests. Repeat option for various cookies.
	-d postdata               : Use post data (ex: "id=FUZZ&catalogue=1")
	-H header                 : Use header (ex:"Cookie:id=1312321&user=FUZZ"). Repeat option for various headers.
	--basic/ntlm/digest auth  : in format "user:pass" or "FUZZ:FUZZ" or "domain\FUZ2Z:FUZZ"
	
	--hc/hl/hw/hh N[,N]+      : Hide responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
	--sc/sl/sw/sh N[,N]+      : Show responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
	--ss/hs regex             : Show/hide responses with the specified regex within the content
	--filter <filter>         : Show/hide responses using the specified filter expression (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
	--prefilter <filter>      : Filter items before fuzzing using the specified expression. Repeat for concatenating filters.

default
default
********************************************************
* Wfuzz 3.1.0 - The Web Fuzzer                         *
*                                                      *
* Version up to 1.4c coded by:                         *
* Christian Martorella (cmartorella@edge-security.com) *
* Carlos del ojo (deepbit@gmail.com)                   *
*                                                      *
* Version 1.4d to 3.1.0 coded by:                      *
* Xavier Mendez (xmendez@edge-security.com)            *
********************************************************

Usage:	wfuzz [options] -z payload,params <url>

	FUZZ, ..., FUZnZ  wherever you put these keywords wfuzz will replace them with the values of the specified payload.
	FUZZ{baseline_value} FUZZ will be replaced by baseline_value. It will be the first request performed and could be used as a base for filtering.


Options:
	-h                        : This help
	--help                    : Advanced help
	--version                 : Wfuzz version details
	-e <type>                 : List of available encoders/payloads/iterators/printers/scripts
	
	-c                        : Output with colors
	-v                        : Verbose information.
	--interact                : (beta) If selected,all key presses are captured. This allows you to interact with the program.
	
	-p addr                   : Use Proxy in format ip:port:type. Repeat option for using various proxies.
	                            Where type could be SOCKS4,SOCKS5 or HTTP if omitted.
	
	-t N                      : Specify the number of concurrent connections (10 default)
	-s N                      : Specify time delay between requests (0 default)
	-R depth                  : Recursive path discovery being depth the maximum recursion level (0 default)
	-D depth                  : Maximum link depth level (4 default)
	-L, --follow              : Follow HTTP redirections
	
	-u url                    : Specify a URL for the request.
	-z payload                : Specify a payload for each FUZZ keyword used in the form of type,parameters,encoder.
	                            A list of encoders can be used, ie. md5-sha1. Encoders can be chained, ie. md5@sha1.
	                            Encoders category can be used. ie. url
	                            Use help as a payload to show payload plugin's details (you can filter using --slice)
	-w wordlist               : Specify a wordlist file (alias for -z file,wordlist).
	-V alltype                : All parameters bruteforcing (allvars and allpost). No need for FUZZ keyword.
	-X method                 : Specify an HTTP method for the request, ie. HEAD or FUZZ
	
	-b cookie                 : Specify a cookie for the requests
	-d postdata               : Use post data (ex: "id=FUZZ&catalogue=1")
	-H header                 : Use header (ex:"Cookie:id=1312321&user=FUZZ")
	--basic/ntlm/digest auth  : in format "user:pass" or "FUZZ:FUZZ" or "domain\FUZ2Z:FUZZ"
	
	--hc/hl/hw/hh N[,N]+      : Hide responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
	--sc/sl/sw/sh N[,N]+      : Show responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB for taking values from baseline)
	--ss/hs regex             : Show/Hide responses with the specified regex within the content
local_help.help:
/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/wfuzz/__init__.py:34: UserWarning:Pycurl is not compiled against Openssl. Wfuzz might not work correctly when fuzzing SSL sites. Check Wfuzz's documentation for more information.
 /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/wfuzz/wfuzz.py:78: UserWarning:Fatal exception: Bad usage: You must specify a payload.
local_help.man:
WFUZZ(1)                         User Commands                         WFUZZ(1)

NAME
     wfuzz - a web application bruteforcer

SYNOPSIS
     wfuzz [options] -z payload,params <url>

OPTIONS
     -h     Print information about available arguments.

     --help
            Advanced help.

     --version
            Wfuzz version details

     -e <type>
            List of available encoders/payloads/iterators/printers/scripts

     --recipe <filename>
            Reads options from a recipe

     --dump-recipe <filename>
            Prints current options as a recipe

     --oF <filename>
            Saves fuzz results to a file. These can be consumed later using the
            wfuzz payload.

     -c     Output with colors

     -v     Verbose information.

     -f filename,printer
            Store  results  in the output file using the specified printer (raw
            printer if omitted).

     -o printer
            Format output using the specified printer.

     --interact
            (beta) If selected, all key presses are captured. This  allows  you
            to interact with the program.

     --dry-run
            Print  the results of applying the requests without actually making
            any HTTP request.

     --prev
            Print the previous HTTP requests (only when using payloads generat-
            ing fuzzresults)

     -p addr
            Use Proxy in format ip:port:type. Repeat option for  using  various
            proxies.  Where type could be SOCKS4, SOCKS5 or HTTP if omitted.

     -t N   Specify the number of concurrent connections (10 default)

     -s N   Specify time delay between requests (0 default)

     -R depth
            Recursive path discovery being depth the maximum recursion level.

     -L, --follow
            Follow HTTP redirections

     -Z     Scan mode (Connection errors will be ignored).

     --req-delay N
            Sets  the  maximum  time  in seconds the request is allowed to take
            (CURLOPT_TIMEOUT). Default 90.

     --conn-delay N
            Sets the maximum time in seconds the connection phase to the server
            to take (CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT). Default 90.

     -A     Alias for --script=default -v -c

     --script=
            Equivalent to --script=default

     --script=<plugins>
            Runs script's scan. <plugins> is a comma  separated  list  of  plu-
            gin-files or plugin-categories

     --script-help=<plugins>
            Show help about scripts.

     --script-args n1=v1,...
            Provide  arguments  to  scripts.  ie.  --script-args grep.regex="<A
            href=\"(.*?)\">"

     -u url
            Specify a URL for the request.

     -m iterator
            Specify an iterator for combining payloads (product by default)

     -z payload
            Specify a payload for  each  FUZZ  keyword  used  in  the  form  of
            type,parameters,encoder.   A  list  of  encoders  can  be used, ie.
            md5-sha1. Encoders can be chained, ie. md5@sha1.  Encoders category
            can be used. ie. url.  Use help as a payload to show  payload  plu-
            gin's details (you can filter using --slice)

     --zP <params>
            Arguments  for  the specified payload (it must be preceded by -z or
            -w).

     --slice <filter>
            Filter payload's elements using the specified expression.  It  must
            be preceded by -z.

     -w wordlist
            Specify a wordlist file (alias for -z file,wordlist).

     -V alltype
            All parameters bruteforcing (allvars and allpost). No need for FUZZ
            keyword.

     -X method
            Specify an HTTP method for the request, ie. HEAD or FUZZ

     -b cookie
            Specify  a cookie for the requests. Repeat option for various cook-
            ies.

     -d postdata
            Use post data (ex: "id=FUZZ&catalogue=1")

     -H headers
            Use headers (ex:"Host:www.mysite.com,Cookie:id=1312321&user=FUZZ").
            Repeat option for various headers.

     --basic/ntlm/digest auth
            in format "user:pass" or "FUZZ:FUZZ" or "domain\FUZ2Z:FUZZ"

     --hc/hl/hw/hh N[,N]+
            Hide responses with the specified code/lines/words/chars  (Use  BBB
            for taking values from baseline)

     --sc/sl/sw/sh N[,N]+
            Show  responses  with the specified code/lines/words/chars (Use BBB
            for taking values from baseline)

     --ss/hs regex
            Show/Hide responses with the specified regex within the content

     --filter <filter>
            Filter responses using the specified expression (Use BBB for taking
            values   from    baseline)    It    should    be    composed    of:
            c,l,w,h/and,or/=,<,>,!=,<=,>=  Keyword:  FUZZ, ..., FUZnZ  wherever
            you put these keywords wfuzz will replace them with the  values  of
            the specified payload.  Baseline: FUZZ{baseline_value} FUZZ will be
            replaced  by baseline_value. It will be the first request performed
            and could be used as a base for filtering.

     --prefilter <filter>
            Filter items before fuzzing using the specified expression.

EXAMPLES
            wfuzz -c -z file,users.txt -z file,pass.txt --sc 200 http://www.site.com/log.asp?user=FUZZ&pass=FUZ2Z

            wfuzz -c -z range,1-10 --hc=BBB http://www.site.com/FUZZ{something not there}

            wfuzz --script=robots -z list,robots.txt http://www.webscantest.com/FUZZ

     More examples are available in the README..

wfuzz 2.2.11                       June 2018                           WFUZZ(1)

apt_metadata.description:
Web application bruteforcer
Wfuzz is a tool designed for bruteforcing Web Applications,
it can be used for finding resources not linked
directories, servlets, scripts, etc, bruteforce GET and
POST parameters for checking different kind of injections
(SQL, XSS, LDAP,etc), bruteforce Forms parameters
(User/Password), Fuzzing, etc.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
